required_actions: Derive synchronization predicates systematically from C9 integration contracts — predicate set must be provably exhaustive for all cross-layer reference types defined in C9
Specify C5 VTD hash chain and periodic opinion snapshot mechanism as a concrete protocol (resolving MF-3 design detail)
Define the cross-layer witness corroboration threshold and quorum rules for authority-override scenarios
Fully specify Part III adversarial reconstruction API contracts and data structures even though implementation is deferred to Wave 4+, ensuring Parts I/II are forward-compatible
monitoring_flags: MF-1: Authority-directed reconciliation remains the weakest theoretical link (Confidence 3/5). Monitor whether DESIGN produces a tighter bound on detectable corruption classes.

MF-2: Part III implementation deferral to Wave 4+ — if adversarial digest corruption is observed before Wave 4, system falls back to full snapshot restore. Ensure this is specified as explicit degraded-mode path.
MF-3: Synchronization predicate exhaustiveness — define extensibility mechanism for post-DESIGN C9 contract expansion.
MF-4: C6 reconstruction coverage (~50%) is significantly lower than other layers. DESIGN must document unrecoverable C6 state and acceptable data loss bounds.
MF-5: 36,000-second worst-case consistent-cut gap (one CONSOLIDATION_CYCLE). Verify acceptability for C8 settlement where AIC balances are at stake.
rationale: Research validates all seven scientific questions at Confidence 3-5/5 with no fatal contradictions. Landscape confirms four unoccupied technology gaps. Part III has no direct precedent. Authority-directed reconciliation limitation is fundamental but mitigated. Impact tempered to 3.5 reflecting per-layer recovery coverage of common cases. No unresolvable blockers.
